title: Crushing shortages have pushed long-term supply agreements for SSDs and HDDs to record five years large customers are signing large contracts
summary: Demand for storage devices has reached record highs, prompting large customers to sign long-term supply agreements with major manufacturers like Sandisk, Seagate, and Western Digital, potentially committing to up to five years.
insight: Supply chain commitments are adapting to unprecedented storage demand.

title: AMD Ryzen 9 PRO 9965X3D spotted on Passmark company's first PRO chip with 3D V-Cache also bumps the lineup up to 16 cores
summary: The AMD Ryzen 9 PRO 9965X3D is a 16-core Zen 5 chip with 3D V-Cache, marking the first PRO series processor with this technology. It is based on the 9950X3D and likely has a lower TDP. Three samples of this processor have appeared on PassMark.
insight: First PRO chip with 3D V-Cache technology introduced.

title: Enthusiast builds a PC big enough to live in humans in this RGB-lit fish tank case look just like figurines
summary: A Chinese TechTuber created a massive tower PC for human-sized operations, equipped with air conditioning, allowing users to work and play comfortably inside. The PC resembles a large RGB-lit fish tank case and features a living space for humans. This innovative design pushes the boundaries of traditional PC construction.
insight: Redefining personal computing spaces with giant, functional, self-sustaining PCs.

title: Nvidia accelerates end-of-life for some Jetson AI processors due to memory shortages RAMpocalypse sends older DDR4-based modules to the great scrapheap in the sky
summary: Nvidia is accelerating the end-of-life for some Jetson AI processors due to market forces and memory shortages. This is largely a case of market reality catching up, rather than an abrupt discontinuation. Older DDR4-based modules are being phased out.
insight: Memory shortages drive Nvidia to accelerate AI processor end-of-life

title: Save $370 on this AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition with an X870E motherboard and 32GB of RAM big savings for your next high-end build
summary: This Newegg bundle combines AMD's flagship CPU with high-end components at a discounted price, offering cutting-edge performance with practical value.
insight: High-end components at a discounted price for significant savings opportunities.

title: A suspected YouTube interface bug spikes RAM usage above 7 gigabytes, users report severe lag and frozen tabs bug might be trapping browsers in an endless layout loop
summary: YouTube users report severe lag and frozen tabs due to a suspected UI bug, which causes browsers to consume massive amounts of RAM. The issue is thought to be triggered by endless layout recalculations. Developers are working to resolve the problem.
insight: Endless layout loops can cripple browser performance and RAM.

title: Popular 90s search engine Ask Jeeves finally bites the dust parent company shutters website that pioneered natural language queries, only a placeholder results page remains
summary: Ask Jeeves, a pioneering search engine, has been shut down by IAC after 30 years. The website, known for natural language queries, now displays a placeholder results page. This marks the end of an era for search engine innovation.
insight: Legacy internet innovation succumbs to evolving digital search landscape.

title: Anthropic in early talks to buy DRAM-less AI inference chips from UK startup Fractile's SRAM architecture reduces need for pricey memory during extreme pricing and shortage crunch
summary: Anthropic holds talks with Fractile to acquire DRAM-less AI inference chips.
insight: Fractile's SRAM architecture mitigates DRAM shortages and pricing pressures.

title: Chinese court rules companies can't fire workers just because AI is cheaper ruling says automation alone doesn't justify layoffs
summary: Chinese courts ruled against firing workers for cost savings due to AI, citing labor law.
insight: Protecting human workers from AI-driven layoffs is a labor rights priority.

title: Jensen says Nvidia now has 'zero percent' market share in China says US export policy 'has already largely backfired'
summary: Nvidia co-founder Jensen Huang states China is a lost market due to US sanctions. The US export policy has largely backfired, according to Huang. This may lead to the US losing out in the AI competition.
insight: US export policy risks harming national interests in global AI competition.

title: Utah first state to hold websites liable for users who mask their location with VPNs law goes into effect, designed to prevent bypassing age checks
summary: Utah's Online Age Verification Amendments (Senate Bill 73) take effect on May 6, making it the first state to hold websites liable for users who mask their location with VPNs to bypass age checks.
insight: Age verification online regulations gain traction in the US.

title: Keychain-size GameCube uses genuine Nintendo silicon system also includes a dock, design shared to GitHub
summary: A miniature GameCube keychain has been created by modders, featuring genuine Nintendo silicon. The project, known as Nintendo Kawaii, includes a matching dock. Design files have been shared on GitHub.
insight: Advancements in miniaturization enable innovative retro gaming projects.

title: Amazon s Middle East data centers damaged by Iran drone and missile attacks will be down for several months during repairs U.S. and Iran currently observing an uneasy truce, but renewed strikes possible if talks break down
summary: Amazon's Middle East data centers in Bahrain and UAE are down for several months due to damage from Iran drone and missile attacks. The company has suspended billing for affected customers and recommended alternative regions. Repairs will take time, and a renewed conflict could exacerbate the issue.
insight: Downtime and security threats impact global e-commerce and cloud services significantly.

title: Japan is deploying ultra-cheap cardboard drones built for swarm warfare and expendable combat missions $2,000 expendable combat drones cost less than some gaming PCs
summary: Japan is deploying low-cost cardboard drones for swarm warfare and expendable combat missions. The AirKamuy 150 costs around $2,000, making it a cost-effective option for military operations. These drones are designed for mass production and disposal.
insight: Low-cost drones enable more flexible, expendable military strategies.

title: Boardcon PICOT536 SoM and EMT536 SBC feature Allwinner T536 Edge AI processor
summary: Boardcon's PICOT536 SoM and EMT536 SBC feature the Allwinner T536 Edge AI processor, suitable for industrial HMI, machine vision, and robotics applications. The module is equipped with a quad-core Arm Cortex-A55 CPU, RISC-V coprocessors, and a 2 TOPS NPU for edge AI processing.
insight: Advances edge AI computing capabilities in industrial automation applications.

title: AMD Versal Prime VM2152 mid-range adaptive SoC features 112 Gbps GTM Transceivers, 600Gbps Ethernet, DDR5/LPDDR5
summary: AMD's Versal Prime VM2152 is a mid-range adaptive SoC offering high-speed connectivity, 112 Gbps transceivers, and 600 Gbps Ethernet, balancing programmable logic with hardened IP blocks for power-efficient performance.
insight: High-speed connectivity with power efficiency for wired comms and aerospace applications.

title: Steam Survey: AMD's CPU gains stall as Nvidia's final RTX 5000 desktop GPU appears
summary: Nvidia's RTX 3060 remains the most popular GPU among Steam survey participants.
insight: Nvidia's GPU dominance unmoved by AMD's CPU gains.

title: A startup says it can replace home sprinklers with sound waves. Fire safety experts say show us the data
summary: A startup claims sound waves can replace home sprinklers for fire safety, but experts demand evidence.
insight: Acoustic fire suppression has been studied for years with existing data.

title: This $170 retro-style dock gives your Mac mini a tiny screen and upgradeable storage
summary: Wokyis offers a retro-styled dock for Mac mini with NVMe storage and extra ports.
insight: Enhances Mac mini's functionality without sacrificing its compact design.

title: Japan is building military drones out of cardboard, and they're faster and cheaper than you'd expect
summary: Japan's defense ministry is partnering with Air Kamuy to develop cardboard drones. These drones are reportedly faster and cheaper than expected. This collaboration aims to make Japan a leader in low-cost drone production.
insight: Cardboard drones challenge traditional materials in modern warfare applications.

title: The Sega Genesis is the latest classic console to get the Lego treatment
summary: The Lego Sega Genesis console model will be available on June 1, priced at $39.99. The set comprises 479 pieces, allowing for a compact yet detailed replica of the original console. It measures 4.5 inches long and six inches wide.
insight: Nostalgic gamers can relive retro console memories with Lego models
